updates.title: Anthropic Confidentially Files for IPO, Setting Up Public-Market Test of AI Boom
updates.summary: Anthropic confirmed it has confidentially submitted a draft Form S-1 registration statement to the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission, taking its first formal step toward an initial public offering. The filing comes days after the company closed a $65 billion Series H funding round at a $965 billion valuation and disclosed a $47 billion annual revenue run rate. Anthropic stated the offering will depend on market conditions and that it has not yet decided on the number of shares or price range, positioning itself ahead of rival OpenAI in the race to go public.
updates.accuracy_notes: Anthropic issued an official company statement confirming the filing. Multiple tier-1 outlets (CNBC, TechCrunch, NPR, Washington Post, NBC News) reported identical details on June 1, 2026, all citing Anthropic's announcement. The $965B valuation and $47B ARR figures are consistent across all sources. The Verge is confirmed as one of the original reporters via third-party aggregators. High factual confidence.

updates.title: Claude Code v2.1.160 — Security Hardening, WSL Clipboard Fix, and 'Ultracode' Keyword Rename
updates.summary: Anthropic released Claude Code v2.1.160 (commit bdb04fc) on June 2, 2026 at 02:10 UTC, bringing a batch of security and stability improvements. Key changes include confirmation prompts before writing to shell startup files (.zshenv, .bash_login, etc.) and build-tool config files (.npmrc, .bazelrc, .devcontainer/), a fix for the WSL clipboard using PowerShell interop instead of OSC 52, session restoration fixes for background agents, and renaming the workflow trigger keyword from 'workflow' to 'ultracode'. CJK IME positioning in the agents view and voice mode with non-ASCII directory names were also fixed.
updates.accuracy_notes: Sourced directly from the official github.com/anthropics/claude-code/releases page, which mirrors docs.anthropic.com/en/release-notes/claude-code. Commit hash bdb04fc and timestamp (June 2, 2026 02:10 UTC) are verifiable. All listed changes are specific engineering claims that can be independently confirmed by inspecting the release diff. High factual confidence.
